REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS GARO OUSTAYAN AND DIAMOND BROKERS OF LOS ALTOS, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. PORTION OF THE COMPLAINT PERTAINING TO UNIT 1929 AND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SHALL PROCEED IN ARBITRATIONS. PARTIES SHALL PROMPTLY SELECT AN ARBITRATOR AND ARE STRONGLY ENCOURAGED TO COMPLETE THE ARBITRATION BEFORE JANUARY 30, 2023 TRIAL. COMPLAINT REGARDING UNIT 1931 SHALL PROCEED IN COURT. THE COURT NOTES THAT MR. OUSTAYAN AND HIS COUNSEL ACTED INCONSISTENTLY WITH AN EXISTENCE OF AN ENFORCEABLE ARBITRATION CLAUSE STARTING AT LEAST IN JANUARY 2022. HOWEVER, THIS BEHAVIOR DID NOT AMOUNT TO WAIVER. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT UNDER EXISTING AUTHORITIES AND THE FACTS OF THIS CASE THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS ENFORCEABLE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-27-2023 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-02-28 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Supporting Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Supporting Declarations Of Garo Oustayan And James A. Quadra Request For Judicial Notice Proposed Order Granting Motion
Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Rebecca Coll; Quadra & Coll, LLP; 415-426-3502; appearing on behalf of defendants Garo Oustayan and Diamond Brokers of Los Altos, Inc.
Luke Garvey; Colla & Ray LLP; 415-579-1414; appearing on behalf of plaintiffs 1929 Union LLC and 1929-1931 Union Street Apartments.

Plaintiffs contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS GARO OUSTAYAN AND DIAMOND BROKERS OF LOS ALTOS, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is GRANTED. Portion of the complaint pertaining to unit 1929 and the cross-complaint shall proceed in Arbitrations. Parties shall promptly select an arbitrator and are strongly encouraged to complete the arbitration before January 30, 2023 trial. Complaint regarding unit 1931 shall proceed in Court. The Court notes that Mr. Oustayan and his counsel acted inconsistently with an existence of an enforceable arbitration clause starting at least in January 2022. However, this behavior did not amount to waiver. The Court further notes that under existing authorities and the facts of this case the arbitration clause is enforceable.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
